new_classes.definition_en: A spirituous beverage produced by blending two or more single malt whiskies distilled at different distilleries, without any grain whisky component. Blended malt Scotch whisky is the Scotch variety thereof.
A blended malt whisky produced exclusively in Scotland by blending two or more single malt Scotch whiskies, each distilled at a different distillery, without any grain whisky component.

new_classes.label_en: Blended Malt Whisky in containers ≤ 2 L (CN 2208 30 41)
new_classes.label_de: Blended Malt Whisky in Behältern ≤ 2 L (KN 2208 30 41)
new_classes.definition_en: Blended malt whisky presented in containers of a nominal volume not exceeding 2 litres, as distinguished from CN 2208 30 49 which covers containers of more than 2 litres.
A production process in which two or more single malt whiskies distilled at different distilleries are combined to produce a blended malt whisky.

A quality borne by a blended malt whisky indicating that its constituent single malt whiskies originate from two or more distinct distilleries, discriminating it from single malt whisky (single distillery origin).

coverage_explanation:
The axioms capture the core distinguishing criteria for CN 2208 30 41:
1. ✅ Blended malt whisky is modelled as a SpiritousBeverage produced by a BlendingProcess.
2. ✅ The multi-distillery origin quality (≥ 2 distilleries) distinguishes blended malt from single malt (which bears SingleDistilleryOriginQuality); a complement restriction enforces the disjunction.
3. ✅ The small-container restriction (≤ 2 L via SmallContainer) distinguishes CN 2208 30 41 from its sibling CN 2208 30 49 (containers > 2 L).
4. ✅ Blended malt Scotch whisky is modelled as a subclass with a Scottish-origin specialisation.
5. ⚠️ The legal text refers specifically to "single malt Scotch whiskies" as the blending components; the relationship between BlendingProcess and its input SingleMaltWhisky constituents is not fully axiomatised in OWL (would require n-ary relations or role chains not available in OWL 2 DL without punning).
6. ⚠️ The maturation/ageing requirements typical for Scotch whisky classification (e.g., minimum 3-year oak cask maturation under EU Regulation 2019/787) are not captured, as they are not stated in the provided legal note text.
7. ⚠️ The MaltedBarleyMash class exists in the TBox and could be used to constrain the feedstock of the constituent single malts, but linking it through the blending process would over-engineer the model beyond what the note text requires.
